metadata.rulesText: Tap to draw a card from your library. Each time you use this ability, you must choose one of your artifacts in play and place it in the graveyard. This artifact cannot be one that is already on its way to the graveyard, and artifact creatures killed this way may not be regenerated.

metadata.rulesText: 1: You gain 1 life every time one of your artifacts goes to the graveyard. Can only give 1 life each time an artifact reaches the graveyard.

customAttributes.description: 1: You gain 1 life every time one of your artifacts goes to the graveyard. Can only give 1 life each time an artifact reaches the graveyard.

metadata.rulesText: 3: Select a creature in play; that creature is considered out of play as long as Coffin remains tapped. Hence the creature cannot be the target of spells and cannot receive damage, use special powers, attack, or defend. All counters and enchantments on the creature remain but are also out of play. If Coffin is untapped or removed, creature returns to play tapped. You may choose not to untap Coffin during the untap phase.

customAttributes.description: 3: Select a creature in play; that creature is considered out of play as long as Coffin remains tapped. Hence the creature cannot be the target of spells and cannot receive damage, use special powers, attack, or defend. All counters and enchantments on the creature remain but are also out of play. If Coffin is untapped or removed, creature returns to play tapped. You may choose not to untap Coffin during the untap phase.

formattedAttributes.Description: 3: Select a creature in play; that creature is considered out of play as long as Coffin remains tapped. Hence the creature cannot be the target of spells and cannot receive damage, use special powers, attack, or defend. All counters and enchantments on the creature remain but are also out of play. If Coffin is untapped or removed, creature returns to play tapped. You may choose not to untap Coffin during the untap phase.

metadata.rulesText: 2: Make a creature of power no greater than 2 unblockable by all creatures except artifact creatures until end of turn. Other cards may be used to increase target creature’s power beyond 2 after defense is chosen.

metadata.rulesText: FlyingTetravus gets three +1/+1 counters when cast. During your upkeep, you may move each of these counters on or off Tetravus. Counters moved off of Tetravus become independent 1/1 flying artifact creatures. If such a creature dies, the counter is removed from play. Such creatures may not have enchantments cast on them, and they do not share any enchantments on Tetravus.

customAttributes.description: FlyingTetravus gets three +1/+1 counters when cast. During your upkeep, you may move each of these counters on or off Tetravus. Counters moved off of Tetravus become independent 1/1 flying artifact creatures. If such a creature dies, the counter is removed from play. Such creatures may not have enchantments cast on them, and they do not share any enchantments on Tetravus.

formattedAttributes.Description: FlyingTetravus gets three +1/+1 counters when cast. During your upkeep, you may move each of these counters on or off Tetravus. Counters moved off of Tetravus become independent 1/1 flying artifact creatures. If such a creature dies, the counter is removed from play. Such creatures may not have enchantments cast on them, and they do not share any enchantments on Tetravus.

metadata.rulesText: If opponent has fewer than three cards in hand during his or her upkeep, the Rack does 1 damage to opponent for each card fewer than three.

customAttributes.description: If opponent has fewer than three cards in hand during his or her upkeep, the Rack does 1 damage to opponent for each card fewer than three.

formattedAttributes.Description: If opponent has fewer than three cards in hand during his or her upkeep, the Rack does 1 damage to opponent for each card fewer than three.

metadata.rulesText: All non-creature artifacts in play lose all their usual abilities and become artifact creatures with toughness and power both equal to their casting costs. If Titania’s Song leaves play, artifacts return to normal just before the untap phase of the next turn.

customAttributes.description: All non-creature artifacts in play lose all their usual abilities and become artifact creatures with toughness and power both equal to their casting costs. If Titania’s Song leaves play, artifacts return to normal just before the untap phase of the next turn.

formattedAttributes.Description: All non-creature artifacts in play lose all their usual abilities and become artifact creatures with toughness and power both equal to their casting costs. If Titania’s Song leaves play, artifacts return to normal just before the untap phase of the next turn.

metadata.rulesText: Search through your library for one artifact and immediately place it into play; also, choose any artifact in play that you control and place it in its owner’s graveyard. If the new artifact has a casting cost greater than that of the discarded one, you must pay the difference or Transmute Artifact fails and both artifacts are discarded. Shuffle your library after playing this card.

customAttributes.description: Search through your library for one artifact and immediately place it into play; also, choose any artifact in play that you control and place it in its owner’s graveyard. If the new artifact has a casting cost greater than that of the discarded one, you must pay the difference or Transmute Artifact fails and both artifacts are discarded. Shuffle your library after playing this card.

formattedAttributes.Description: Search through your library for one artifact and immediately place it into play; also, choose any artifact in play that you control and place it in its owner’s graveyard. If the new artifact has a casting cost greater than that of the discarded one, you must pay the difference or Transmute Artifact fails and both artifacts are discarded. Shuffle your library after playing this card.
formattedAttributes.TCGplayer Tip: With only one printing in 1994 and its position on the Reserved List, Transmute Artifact is highly collectable.

metadata.rulesText: First strike, counts as a wall.
metadata.flavorText: Even the most conservative generals revised their tactics after the Battle of Sarinth, during which a handful of peasant-pikemen held off a trio of rampaging Craw Wurms.

customAttributes.description: First strike, counts as a wall.
customAttributes.flavorText: Even the most conservative generals revised their tactics after the Battle of Sarinth, during which a handful of peasant-pikemen held off a trio of rampaging Craw Wurms.

metadata.rulesText: All attacking creatures lose -1/-0. Creatures with power less than 1 deal no damage.

customAttributes.description: All attacking creatures lose -1/-0. Creatures with power less than 1 deal no damage.

formattedAttributes.Description: All attacking creatures lose -1/-0. Creatures with power less than 1 deal no damage.
formattedAttributes.TCGplayer Tip: Weakstone is great when you're playing defense and don't care about attacking. Find alternative ways to win, such as mill, planeswalkers or combos, to make the effect essentially one-sided and punish opponents trying to play offensively.

metadata.rulesText: Tap to turn target non-creature artifact into an artifact creature with both power and toughness equal to its casting cost. This transformation lasts until your next upkeep; target retains all its original abilities as well.

customAttributes.description: Tap to turn target non-creature artifact into an artifact creature with both power and toughness equal to its casting cost. This transformation lasts until your next upkeep; target retains all its original abilities as well.

metadata.rulesText: Flying, first strikeDuring your upkeep, choose one of your artifacts in play and place it in the graveyard, or Yawgmoth Demon becomes tapped and deals 2 points of damage to you. Artifact creatures destroyed this way may not be regenerated.

customAttributes.description: Flying, first strikeDuring your upkeep, choose one of your artifacts in play and place it in the graveyard, or Yawgmoth Demon becomes tapped and deals 2 points of damage to you. Artifact creatures destroyed this way may not be regenerated.

metadata.rulesText: Attacking does not cause Yotian Soldier to tap.
metadata.flavorText: After Kroog was destroyed while most of its defenders were at his side, Urza vowed that none of his allies would ever need to fear for their own defense again, even while laying siege to a city far from their homes.

customAttributes.description: Attacking does not cause Yotian Soldier to tap.
